text: Bicyclopentylation of bioactive molecules using abstract rigid BCP reagents Abstract Rigid 1,3-disubstituted bicyclo[1.1.1]pentanes (BCPs) derived from linear bioisosteres of the para -substituted benzene ring of drugs are highly desired due to their favorable pharmacokinetics. Synthesis of abstract rigid BCPs typically involves solution manipulation of the labile [1.1.1]propellane. Unfortunately, stable counterparts lacking the unique reactivity of the versatile propellane core have not been reported so far. We report herein stable thianthrenium-derived BCP reagents that enable efficient O -, N - and C -alkylation of bioactive molecules via bicyclopentylation reactions, surpassing the capacity of [1.1.1]propellane and any other reagent to date. Thianthrenium-derived reactivity and stability in the synthesis of BCP-thianthrenium reagents as well as their reaction with bioactive molecules are explained by the redox and stereoelectronic properties of the thianthrene ring derived from the release of strain and subsequent strain retention. Formation of BCP radicals by selective mesolytic cleavage of the weak exocyclic C–S bond in the reduced thianthrenium species facilitates transition metal-mediated C–O, C–N and C–C bond formations in molecules at almost any stage of complex multistep syntheses while accommodating a wide range of functional groups present.
